---
title: "Muscadine (Vitis rotundifolia)"
slug: "vitis-rotundifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/624014486/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Muscadine"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Vitales"
tax_family: "Vitaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Vitales", "Vitaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/624014486/medium.jpg" alt="Muscadine" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jacksonmartin336">
jacksonmartin336</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342686983">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Vitales
-   **Family:** Vitaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=82529)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in forests, thickets, and along edges of wooded areas.

## Natural history notes

A woody, climbing vine characterized by its thick, rounded leaves and tendrils that allow it to climb over trees and shrubs. In Florida, it is commonly found in forests, thickets, and along edges of wooded areas. It grows during the warmer months and produces dark purple to bronze grapes that provide an important food source for wildlife.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/82529-Vitis-rotundifolia)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/vitis-rotundifolia/)
